product.name: ArWoWear T21 Flame Arc Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Trudnopalne z ochroną przed łukiem elektrycznym
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Antistatic, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, Flame Retardant, Antisatic Fabric , 330 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 330 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T21 Flame Arc Pro is heavy weight 330 gr/m', 79% Cotton %20 Polyester 1% Carbon, vat dyed, flame retardant, antistatic workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
Due to carbon fibers used on the back side of the fabric it has antistatic certification. (EN 1149-5)
Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
It is durable up to 100 washing.
79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, Flame Retardant, Antisatic Fabric , 330 gr/m'2
330 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 79% Cotton (CO) 20% Polyester 1% Carbon(CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp>1200N; Weft>1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>25N; Weft>20N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

matches.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification and molten-metal splash resistance directly support welding/metallurgy use.

evidence: Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
matches.rationale: Explicit electric-arc thermal hazard protection (EN 61482) directly ties the fabric to electrical/energy-sector protective use, beyond generic antistatic or FR claims.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: EN ISO 11612 provides flame/heat protection but the product data never states firefighter or fire-brigade use, only general FR workwear.
No explicit military, police or uniformed-service reference in the supplied data.
